skill.name: minimax-docx
skill.description: Professional DOCX document creation, editing, and formatting using OpenXML SDK (.NET). Three pipelines: (A) create new documents from scratch, (B) fill/edit content in existing documents, (C) apply template formatting with XSD validation gate-check. MUST use this skill whenever the user wants to produce, modify, or format a Word document — including when they say "write a report", "draft a proposal", "make a contract", "fill in this form", "reformat to match this template", or any task whose final output is a .docx file. Even if the user doesn't mention "docx" explicitly, if the task implies a printable/formal document, use this skill.
skill.summary: Professional DOCX document creation, editing, and formatting using OpenXML SDK (.NET). Three pipelin...
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 2894 patterns across 75 files, but all findings are false positives. 'Hardcoded URLs' are OpenXML namespace URIs (schemas.openxmlformats.org) in XML stylesheets and XSD schemas. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches on unrelated documentation text in comments and markdown files. 'C2 keywords' trigger on style IDs like 'TOC2'. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' matches on code snippets in markdown reference documentation. 'System reconnaissance' and 'Network reconnaissance' patterns appear in instructional text within guides. The shell scripts (setup.sh, env_check.sh, setup.ps1) use sudo, /proc, and command substitution legitimately for environment detection and .NET SDK installation. No actual security vulnerabilities, credential exfiltration, or malicious behavior detected.

low_findings.title: Shell Scripts with Elevated Privileges
low_findings.description: setup.sh uses sudo for package installation and system configuration. Legitimate for developer tooling setup but requires user trust.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Sudo commands are used for apt-get install and system-level setup. Arguments are hardcoded, no injection vectors.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution in Shell Scripts
low_findings.description: Multiple shell scripts execute external commands (dotnet, uname, grep, wget). All arguments are hardcoded constants for legitimate tooling purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All command arguments are hardcoded strings for tooling operations. No user input passed to shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs for OpenXML Namespaces
low_findings.description: XML stylesheets and XSD schemas contain hardcoded URLs. These are OpenXML namespace declarations (schemas.openxmlformats.org), not external network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are namespace declarations in XML/XSD, not fetch or HTTP calls. Standard OpenXML practice.

content.user_title: Create and Edit DOCX Documents with OpenXML
content.value_statement: Creating professional Word documents programmatically is error-prone and time-consuming. This skill provides three pipelines for DOCX creation, editing, and template application using the OpenXML SDK with built-in validation.
actual_capabilities: Create new DOCX documents from scratch with professional formatting (reports, letters, memos, academic papers)
Edit existing DOCX documents: replace text, fill placeholders, update tables, modify sections
Apply template formatting to existing documents with style merging and structure transfer
Validate document structure against OpenXML XSD schemas and business rule constraints
Generate complex layouts: multi-section documents, headers/footers, tables of contents, footnotes, track changes
Support CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) typography with proper font mapping and GB/T 9704 compliance
limitations: Requires .NET SDK 8.0 or later installed on the system
Does not support legacy .doc format (must convert to .docx first)
Shell-based setup scripts require bash (Linux/macOS/WSL) or PowerShell (Windows)
Complex template merging with conflicting styles may require manual C# intervention

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate formatted business reports, academic papers, or conference submissions with consistent styling from structured data.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts and researchers who need to produce formatted documents regularly

use_cases.title: Document Template Application
use_cases.description: Apply corporate or institutional templates to existing documents, ensuring brand compliance and style consistency across teams.
use_cases.target_user: Office workers who must format documents according to organizational standards

use_cases.title: CJK Thesis and Official Document Creation
use_cases.description: Create Chinese government documents (公文), university theses, and CJK-formatted documents with proper typography and compliance.
use_cases.target_user: Students and government workers in CJK regions requiring standards-compliant documents

prompt_templates.title: Create a Formatted Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a business report titled 'Q4 Sales Analysis' with the author name 'John Smith'. Use A4 page size with standard margins. Include a table of contents, three sections (Executive Summary, Data Analysis, Conclusions), and a footer with page numbers. Use a professional corporate style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pipeline A: Creating a new document from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Fill Placeholders in a Template
prompt_templates.description: Fill in document placeholders with user data
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a contract template at contract_template.docx. Fill in the placeholders: {{client_name}} with 'Acme Corp', {{date}} with '2025-03-15', and {{amount}} with '$50,000'. Save the result as acme_contract.docx.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pipeline B: Editing content in an existing document

prompt_templates.title: Apply Corporate Template Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a draft document at draft.docx and a corporate template at corporate_template.docx. Apply the template styling to my draft while preserving all the content. Validate against the business rules XSD and run the gate-check.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pipeline C: Applying template formatting to existing content

prompt_templates.title: Create a Chinese Government Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Chinese government document (公文) following GB/T 9704 standards. The document should use the correct fonts (FZXiaoBiaoSong for title, FangSong_GB2312 for body), proper heading hierarchy, and standard government layout with red header line.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pipeline A: CJK-specific document creation with regulatory compliance

output_examples.input: Create a memo with title 'Team Update', author 'Manager', plain text content with two sections
output: Document created: output.docx with memo style
Result: Professional memo document with proper heading styles and section formatting

output_examples.input: Apply corporate template to existing draft document
output: Template styles merged with source content
Result: Draft document now styled with corporate branding, headers, and footers

best_practices: Always run the validation pipeline (merge-runs, XSD validate, business rules validate) after every write operation to prevent document corruption
Read the relevant Samples/*.cs file before writing C# code to use proven, SDK-version-verified OpenXML patterns
Use Pipeline C (Base-Replace) for templates with complex multi-section headers and footers to preserve section structure intact
anti_patterns: Do not recreate headers and footers from scratch when applying templates, copy template header and footer XML to preserve section settings
Do not insert empty paragraphs as section separators, use section break properties and style spacing for visual separation instead
Do not skip the gate-check validation for Scenario C operations, it catches structural issues that cause document corruption in Word

faq.question: Do I need to install .NET SDK to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, .NET SDK 8.0 or later is required. Run bash scripts/setup.sh or powershell scripts/setup.ps1 to install dependencies automatically.

faq.question: Can this skill edit legacy .doc files?
faq.answer: No, this skill works with .docx files only. Use scripts/doc_to_docx.sh to convert .doc files to .docx format first.

faq.question: What types of documents can this skill create?
faq.answer: Reports, letters, memos, academic papers, government documents, theses, contracts, proposals, and any document type supported by the OpenXML standard.

faq.question: How do I apply a corporate template to an existing document?
faq.answer: Use Pipeline C with the apply-template CLI command. For complex templates with multi-section headers, use C-2 Base-Replace mode which copies the template as the output base and replaces content.

faq.question: Does this skill support Chinese, Japanese, and Korean text?
faq.answer: Yes, full CJK support with proper font mapping, named font sizes, and GB/T 9704 compliance for Chinese government documents. Read references/cjk_typography.md for details.

faq.question: What happens if XSD validation fails?
faq.answer: Run the auto-repair command (fix-order) to fix element ordering issues, then re-validate. If validation still fails, fall back to business rules validation and preview the document manually.
